timeline.heading: The Prescott Trail
timeline.intro: Tap any marker to follow the town's story, year by year.
timeline.trailEnd: The trail continues today — Prescott's story is still being written.

milestones.title: Gold Along Granite Creek
milestones.body: A prospecting party led by Joseph Walker found gold in the creeks of the Bradshaw Mountains, drawing the first wave of miners into the area that would become Prescott.

milestones.title: Arizona's First Capital
milestones.body: The newly formed Arizona Territory needed a seat of government. Officials gathered and settled on the name “Prescott,” honoring historian William Hickling Prescott, and the town became the territory's first capital.

milestones.title: Capital Moves to Tucson
milestones.body: Political winds shifted, and the territorial capital was relocated south to Tucson, slowing Prescott's growth as a government center for a decade.

milestones.title: The Capital Returns
milestones.body: Prescott won back its status as territorial capital, restoring the town as the political heart of Arizona for another twelve years.

milestones.title: The World's Oldest Rodeo
milestones.body: Cowboys and ranch hands competed in what would become known as the World's Oldest Rodeo, a Prescott tradition that continues to draw crowds every summer.

milestones.title: Capital Moves to Phoenix
milestones.body: The territorial capital left Prescott for good, settling in Phoenix as the territory's population and growth shifted southward.

milestones.title: The Whiskey Row Fire
milestones.body: A devastating fire tore through downtown, destroying the wooden storefronts of Whiskey Row. Rebuilding began almost immediately - this time in brick and stone.

milestones.title: Elks Opera House Opens
milestones.body: A grand new theater opened downtown, bringing vaudeville, film, and live performance to the rebuilt city - it still hosts shows today.

milestones.title: Hassayampa Inn Opens
milestones.body: This elegant hotel opened its doors downtown, quickly becoming a gathering place for travelers, ranchers, and locals alike.

milestones.title: Sharlot Hall Museum Founded
milestones.body: Pioneer and historian Sharlot Hall opened a museum in the old Territorial Governor's Mansion, dedicating herself to preserving Prescott's frontier history for future generations.
